name: Express REST API + MCP Server Framework
what_it_does: Express REST API + MCP Server Framework combining an Express.js REST API with an MCP server in a single Node.js application — providing boilerplate for building services that expose both HTTP REST endpoints and MCP tools from the same codebase, enabling developers to build dual-interface services for AI agents and traditional API consumers.
use_cases: Building services with both REST API and MCP interfaces from full-stack developers
Migrating existing Express APIs to also support MCP from integration teams
Creating backend services that serve both traditional apps and AI agents
Bootstrapping new TypeScript projects with MCP and REST from teams
Building internal tools with REST for dashboards and MCP for agents
Learning how to combine Express and MCP in a single Node.js service
not_for: Teams only needing MCP (framework adds Express overhead without benefit)
Production-ready server (it's a boilerplate/framework starting point)
best_when: A TypeScript/Node.js developer needs to build a service that exposes both a REST API and MCP tools from the same application.
avoid_when: You only need an MCP server — use simpler MCP-only templates. Or if you only need REST — use standard Express boilerplate.

auth.notes: Framework includes basic auth middleware. REST API and MCP auth implementation is developer responsibility.

pricing.notes: Free boilerplate. Self-hosted. No external service dependencies in core.

known_agent_gotchas: Boilerplate — you must implement your own tools and endpoints
Express and MCP transports use different server mechanisms — test both carefully
Community framework from individual contributor — limited support
Authentication must be added for production use — not production-ready out of the box
Node.js version compatibility should be checked
Running both REST and MCP from same process requires careful error handling
agent_readiness.security_notes: Framework — developer implements security. Add proper auth before production deployment. HTTPS required for production. Separate REST and MCP authorization policies.

security_score_components.security_notes: Framework — implement auth before production. HTTPS required. Separate REST/MCP auth policies.
